claim: Global Mangrove Watch estimates Southeast Asia has lost over 30 percent of mangroves since 1980, primarily to aquaculture conversion, with annual 25m resolution maps available from 1996.
method: Reviewed GMW methodology, mangrove-extent maps, and regional loss statistics.
limitations: 25m resolution may miss narrow fringing mangroves.
Aquaculture attribution relies on land-use classification which has errors.

claim: A 2020 Nature study valued global mangrove ecosystem services at over $1.6 trillion, with flood protection as the largest component, and estimated mangroves reduce annual flood damages by $65 billion.

source.title: The global flood protection benefits of mangroves

method: Reviewed ecosystem-service valuation methodology, flood-protection modeling, and regional breakdowns.
limitations: Protection value depends on storm frequency which varies by location.
